title: Training Logistic Regression
concept: Logistic regression uses gradient descent to adjust weights, bending the S-curve until log loss is minimized.

exercises.prompt: Recap: Why does Log Loss penalize a prediction of 0.01 much more than a prediction of 0.50 if the true label is 1?

options.text: Because it heavily penalizes predictions that are highly confident but incorrect.
Because 0.01 is closer to the true label.
Because Log Loss only works for predictions below 0.50.

exercises.explain: Perfect! Log Loss scales exponentially for confident errors, giving a massive penalty to a prediction of 0.01 when the actual class is 1.

cards.title: Tuning the guitar
cards.body: When you tune a guitar, you turn the peg back and forth, listening to the sound. If the pitch is too low, you turn it up. If it's too high, you turn it down. You make small, calculated adjustments until the sound is perfect.

cards.title: Training Logistic Regression
cards.body: Training logistic regression is the process of using Gradient Descent to iteratively adjust the weights of the model to minimize the total Log Loss.

cards.title: Bending the S-curve
cards.body: Think of the model as a flexible rubber S-curve. The weights control how steep the S-curve is and where it shifts left or right. Training is like using your hands to bend and slide the curve until it splits the data points perfectly.

cards.title: The spam filter updates
cards.body: A spam filter starts with random weights. When it sees spam with a predicted probability of 0.10, the gradient of Log Loss indicates a high error. Gradient descent pushes the weights to increase this probability closer to 1.0.

cards.title: No magic formula
cards.body: A common myth is that we can calculate the perfect weights for logistic regression instantly using a single mathematical formula. In reality, we must use an iterative solver like Gradient Descent because there is no closed-form solution.

cards.title: Training Phase Adjustments
cards.body: How Gradient Descent shapes the classification boundary:

left.text:
Changing weights tilts the slope.
Changing the intercept shifts the S-curve left or right.

right.text:
Weights adjust step-by-step.
Stops when Log Loss is at its lowest possible value.

exercises.prompt: True or false: Unlike linear regression, logistic regression has no direct closed-form formula to calculate the best weights instantly.
exercises.explain: True! We must use an iterative optimizer like Gradient Descent to find the weights that minimize Log Loss.

exercises.prompt: What role does gradient descent play in training a logistic regression model?

options.text: It iteratively updates the weights in the direction that decreases the Log Loss.
It automatically deletes corrupted data points from the dataset.
It converts classification problems into regression problems.

exercises.explain: Exactly! Gradient descent calculates the slopes of the loss function and updates the weights step-by-step to reach the minimum loss.

exercises.sentence: We train a logistic regression model by using gradient descent to find weights that minimize ___.
exercises.explain: Spot on! The goal of training is to find the weights that produce the lowest possible Log Loss.

exercises.prompt: Which of the following represents a step in training a logistic regression model?

options.text: Calculating the gradient of Log Loss and nudging the weights in the opposite direction
Setting all model weights to zero and stopping
Averaging all input values to predict a single constant number

exercises.explain: Correct! We calculate the gradient of the loss function and subtract a fraction of it from the weights to step downhill.

exercises.prompt: You are optimizing a loan default model. Currently, the model predicts low probabilities of default for customers who actually end up defaulting. What will gradient descent do during the next training iteration?

options.text: It will compute gradients of the Log Loss and adjust the weights to increase the predicted probability of default for those customers.
It will automatically change the default outcomes from 1 to 0.
It will increase the learning rate to skip the training process entirely.

exercises.explain: Spectacular! Gradient descent calculates the error gradients and shifts the weights to correct the S-curve, raising predicted probabilities for actual defaults. Congratulations on completing Unit 18! You've mastered logistic regression and classification training!
